# Source Card: Comprehensive pediatric reference intervals for 79 hematology markers in the CALIPER cohort (Mindray BC-6800Plus)

## Summary
This CALIPER study (Bohn et al., Int J Lab Hematol 2023) establishes age- and sex-specific pediatric reference intervals for 79 hematology (CBC/differential/reticulocyte) parameters on the Mindray BC-6800Plus analyzer in 687 healthy children and adolescents (30 days–18 years), per CLSI EP28-A3c. For the pediatric CDS module it is a primary source that (a) justifies age- and sex-partitioned CBC interpretation and (b) supplies the methodology and cohort provenance — but the actual numeric reference-interval bounds live in paywalled full-text tables and are recorded here as GAPs.

- (ev_002) RIs derived per CLSI EP28-A3c — locator: Abstract Methods — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:b2a08558c3733f1409d0f808d1a77ef0fc0f1cecccee4b5ee12f49e099b89195]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: healthy Canadian children and adolescents, 30 days to 18 years, both sexes (CALIPER cohort, n=687).
- Assay/method dependence: all reference intervals are specific to the Mindray BC-6800Plus analyzer; they are NOT directly transferable to other hematology platforms without validation. Analyzer method matters for CDS threshold binding.
- Age structure: partitioning changes concentrated in infancy and at puberty onset (52/79 parameters age-partitioned).
- Sex structure: 11 erythrocyte parameters require separate male/female intervals from puberty.
- Lifecycle: published 2023-03; methodology CLSI EP28-A3c; no retire date; supersedes nothing stated.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- None directly. This source defines reference (health-associated) intervals, not red-flag/referral triggers or treatment thresholds. Values outside a given age/sex RI flag "outside expected range" for interpretation only — the CDS converter must not treat any RI bound as a diagnostic, treatment, or transfusion directive.

## Limitations & conflicts
- GAP (numeric RI cutoffs not locatable): The verbatim per-parameter reference-interval lower/upper limits by age band and sex (e.g., hemoglobin, RBC, hematocrit, MCV, MCHC in g/dL, 10*12/L, %, fL, etc.) are in paywalled Wiley/IJLH full-text tables and could NOT be retrieved from the accessible abstract. Per the output contract, no numeric hematology threshold is asserted from this card. To bind actual CDS thresholds, retrieve the full-text RI tables (DOI 10.1111/ijlh.14068) or CALIPER database.
- Analyzer-specificity conflict risk: RIs are Mindray BC-6800Plus-specific; do NOT collapse or average against RIs from other analyzers or other CALIPER instrument studies — preserve them as instrument-scoped, distinct intervals.
- Population scope: Canadian CALIPER cohort; generalizability to other populations not established here.
- Missingness note: nucleated RBC and immature granulocyte counts were undetectable in this healthy cohort — absence of a positive value is a health-consistent finding, not a missing measurement; CDS must not infer or fabricate a numeric interval for these.
